title: Meet the Super Semiconductor Stock Obliterating Nvidia, AMD, and Broadcom
text: Aehr Test Systems doesn't manufacture chips themselves; instead, they produce the machines and systems to test chips, whether for EVs, data centers, memory and storage, or other applications. The company specializes in wafer-level testing, where multiple chips are evaluated simultaneously on a large wafer rather than individually. The stock has surged 327% year-to-date in 2026, significantly outperforming major chipmakers like Nvidia, Broadcom, and AMD. Most recently, the company received a record $41 million production order from a hyperscaler customer building AI accelerators. Current valuation shows the stock trading at approximately 62 times sales, which the author describes as way overvalued. Wall Street analysts have a median price target of $67 per share, suggesting potential 24% downside from current levels. While roughly 60% of analysts maintain a buy rating based on anticipated 71% revenue growth in fiscal 2027, the author suggests waiting for a better entry point rather than chasing the recent rally.
